bio:
In 2016, I bought my first Bitcoin and Ethereum on a mobile Bread wallet— which I eventually lost access to. Even then, it was obvious that decentralized tech could disrupt and accelerate more than almost any other sector. The draw for me was more than just the technology, though; it was the tangle of complex, high-stakes challenges that offered real value but were impossible to explain without a solid grasp of the engineering, market dynamics, and the unique cultural undercurrents. I’ve always gravitated to those kinds of knotty, real-world problems.

A little while later I dropped out of space engineering at the University of Sydney. The logic was counterintuitive but straightforward: networks that empower people to access, earn, and self-organize, on their terms, had more value to me than any corporate-backed mission to exploit trillion-dollar resources in space “someday.”

From there, I moved into tail-risk algorithmic trading to sharpen my understanding of systemic volatility in markets, real-time. Those years were about grasping how structural risks actually manifest, especially in a volatile environment.

When I launched DIGITALAX in 2020, it was from a future-focused analysis on which areas of engineering, market, and culture would trigger the next paradigm shift. We aimed for fashion and media ecosystems, places where creator agency and aesthetic quality matter, but so does the freedom to act independently. Our tools range from NFTs and decentralized marketplaces to privacy-focused social channels and creative production systems that sidestep traditional structures.

Today, as AI and web3 begin to intersect, I see a natural alignment—both create tools of leverage and give people real power, if done right. After a few years across Dubai, SF, and now based in NYC, my focus is clear: build systems that work meaningfully outside the theory and hype.
